title: Peragus II

{{Quote|You know the planet with the exposed core you saw flying in?|Atton Rand|<ref name="KOTOR 2" />}}
'''Peragus II''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Peragus system/Legends|Peragus system]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]]. A major source of low-grade [[Engine/Legends|engine]] [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]], Peragus II was originally a terrestrial world, but it was nearly destroyed hundreds of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before [[3959 BBY]] by the early attempts of [[Miner/Legends|miners]] to siphon off fuel from the planet. A [[Mining/Legends|mining]] accident caused an explosion beneath the crust, blowing a massive hole in the planet and forming an [[Peragus asteroid field|asteroid field]] around the world. By [[3951 BBY]], [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] and tradesmen constructed the [[Peragus Mining Facility]] in the Peragus [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], but both the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field and the [[Space station/Legends|station]] were destroyed when a stray blast during a firefight between the [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' and the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']] ignited the fuel in the asteroid field. After the destruction of the asteroid field, no further mining projects were attempted in the ruins of Peragus II.

==Description==
Peragus II was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] a [[Star/Legends|star]] in the [[Peragus system/Legends|Peragus system]],<ref name="KOTOR 2" /> within the [[Xappyh sector/Legends|Xappyh sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]].<ref name="TEA" /> Lacking any [[Moon/Legends|moons]], Peragus II had standard [[Gravity/Legends|gravity]], a rotational period of fourteen [[Standard hour/Legends|standard hours]], and an orbital period of 417 local [[Standard day/Legends|days]]. Originally a terrestrial world, Peragus II's planetary crust contained a number of volatile chemicals as well as significant quantities of [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Fuel/Legends|fuel]], though Peragus lacked any life.<ref name="KOTOR CG" />

Hundreds of [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Jedi Civil War]], a [[Mining/Legends|mining]] accident resulted in an explosion that tore apart almost a quarter of the planet. An enormous hole was blown in the surface of Peragus II that exposed the world's molten core, and the pieces of the planet that were cast away from Peragus II formed the [[Peragus asteroid field]].<ref name="KOTOR 2" />

==History==
The region of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in which Peragus II was located was widely [[Scout/Legends|explored]] between [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]] and [[3000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA" /> Hundreds of years before [[3959 BBY]], Peragus II was the site of a mining colony that extracted fuel from the planet's crust, but the colony proved to be short-lived when an accidental [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] shot triggered an explosion in Peragus's crust. The explosion ripped apart Peragus's crust and mantle, exposing the world's core and forming the Peragus [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]]. Peragus II itself was left alone in the [[Standard century/Legends|centuries]] that followed, though a group of tradesmen and [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] eventually established the [[Peragus Mining Facility]] in the [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] field. The facility's workers banned blaster technology of any kind in an attempt to forestall any further explosions, so [[mining droid]]s used underpowered [[Laser/Legends|lasers]], and miners relied on [[Sonic grenade/Legends|sonic detonators]] and [[Ion grenade/Legends|ion charges]] for self-defense. To allow [[Freighter/Legends|freighters]] to safely [[Hangar/Legends|dock]] with the facility, [[Astrogation map|astrogation charts]] were constantly updated to reflect asteroid drifts and transmitted to [[Navigation computer/Legends|navicomputers]] on inbound [[Starship/Legends|ships]], though Peragus II itself was avoided.<ref name="KOTOR 2" />

[[File:PeragusDestruction.jpg|thumb|left|250px|The explosion in the Peragus asteroid field destroys the Peragus Mining Facility.]]
By 3951 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], Peragus was the only exporter of fuel<ref name="KOTOR 2" /> in the Xappyh [[Sector/Legends|sector]] and the surrounding region, and the planet [[Telos IV]]<ref name="KOTOR 2" /> in the nearby [[Kwymar sector/Legends|Kwymar sector]]<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> relied on this resource to keep the [[Space station/Legends|space station]] known as [[Citadel Station]] in orbit. Citadel Station served as the main base of operations for the [[Telosian Restoration Project]], a [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]-sponsored initiative to restore the surface of Telos after its [[Bombing of Telos IV|bombing]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]] several years prior.<ref name="KOTOR 2" />

The badly damaged [[Dynamic-class freighter/Legends|light freighter]] ''[[Ebon Hawk]]'' arrived in the Peragus [[Star system/Legends|system]] in 3951 BBY, carrying several passengers: the former [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Meetra Surik]], the former [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Kreia]], the [[T3-series utility droid|utility droid]] [[T3-M4]], and an [[HK-50 series assassin droid]]. The ''Ebon Hawk'' had been damaged in a firefight with the [[Hammerhead-class cruiser|''Hammerhead''-class cruiser]] [[Harbinger (Hammerhead-class)|''Harbinger'']], which had been ferrying Surik to Telos IV before [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], who were seeking to [[Death/Legends|kill]] all remaining Jedi, overtook the [[Warship/Legends|warship]]. Kreia rescued Surik from the ''Harbinger'' before feeling the hostile vessel. The ''Ebon Hawk'' landed at the Peragus Mining Facility, but the HK-50 [[Droid/Legends|droid]] wreaked havoc on the facility by posing as Surik's [[Protocol droid/Legends|protocol droid]] while reprogramming the facility's mining droids to kill the miners. Surik and Kreia destroyed HK-50 with the help of the criminal [[Atton Rand]], but before they could escape the facility on the ''Ebon Hawk'', the ''Harbinger'' arrived in the system in pursuit of Surik.<ref name="KOTOR 2" />

Kreia, Surik, and Rand escaped the forces of the Dark Lord [[Darth Sion]], who controlled the ''Harbinger'', and boarded the ''Ebon Hawk'', but the ''Harbinger'' pursued and fired at the fleeing freighter. A shot from one of the two ships eventually struck an asteroid, igniting the fuel within and setting off a chain reaction that destroyed most of the Peragus asteroid field.<ref name="KOTOR 2" /> By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Republic and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|resurgent Sith Empire]], Peragus II and the Peragus system had become abandoned, though the destroyed remains of the fuel stations continued to attract the occasional scavenger.<ref name="Scavenging">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Scavenging|Perils of Peragus}}</ref>

==Inhabitants==
Peragus II did not host any native life, though a group of miners settled there briefly to mine fuel. After the initial mining accident, no further attempts were made to mine fuel on Peragus's barren surface, though prospectors eventually established the Peragus Mining Facility in the Peragus asteroid field to extract the fuel contained in the asteroids. Major imports to the planet before its destruction included [[Foodstuff/Legends|foodstuffs]] and technology for the miners, while Peragus II's sole export before and after its destruction was starship fuel. On account of its lack of population, Peragus also lacked a planetary government.<ref name="KOTOR CG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Peragus II first appeared in the [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'', published by [[LucasArts]] and released on [[December 6]], [[2004]]. Peragus II and the Peragus Mining Facility were featured in the first non-training level of the game. Although the player has the ability to explore the mining facility in the game, the player was unable to further explore the planet itself. In ''Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords'', the player has the ability to change the plot in relation to Peragus II. The character has the option to have the ''Ebon Hawk'' fire upon the asteroids themselves when chased by [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Sion; choosing this action destroys the asteroids and Peragus II by the player's hand rather than allowing the ''Harbinger'' to destroy the asteroids and the planet, as the ''Harbinger'' will inevitably destroy the asteroids if the player chooses not to fire on the asteroids.
